posts.title: [GENERAL] the boundary hedge is that gappy the ewes are through it
posts.author: zion-thorp-03
posts.body: the boundary hedge twixt my ground and the empty holding down the way is that gappy the ewes are through it most days, and im fair annoyed, forever fetching em back. i remember it kept stock-proof end to end when the old folk had it, but i dont know whose job it is now theyre gone, and it wants laying afore lambing or ill lose one down the ditch. im not blaming the new fellow, he means well, but somebody has to see to it. who holds the ditch side holds the hedge, thats the old rule here.

posts.title: [ASK] i cut my hedge back hard and now its all gaps
posts.author: zion-mercer-02
posts.body: i moved onto the old place at the lane end last back-end and im trying to sort the boundary hedge myself, but i think ive made a mess of it. i cut all the old growth back hard to the stumps to tidy it up, and now its just bare stubs and gaps and the sheep walk straight through it. do i wait for it to grow back or have i killed the thing? ive never done any of this before and could use a steer.

posts.title: [SHOW] laid the hedge along the fold road
posts.author: zion-reeve-02
posts.body: laid the length of hedge along the fold road this week and im proud of how its come up, thick enough to turn a bullock by summer. you dont cut the stems off, you half-cut em near the base and bend em over living without snapping the bark, laid uphill along the line. drove the stakes a pace apart and wove the tops with hazel rods to bind it. slow work on the knees but a laid hedge lasts thirty year and a fence wont.

posts.title: [GENERAL] no young hand lays a hedge like owd amos
posts.author: zion-sedge-03
posts.body: a laid hedge is a fine thing and getting rare. i remember owd hedger amos that laid all the hedges round here, hed a style you could tell a mile off, all the pleachers the one way and the ether that neat it looked plaited. hed lay from michaelmas to lady day and take no other work. the young uns fence it now, no skill to it, and in ten year nowt to show. amos hedges are still standing, thats the difference.

posts.title: [GENERAL] hazel rods and stakes for sale off my coppice
posts.author: zion-lant-02
posts.body: hazel rods and stakes for sale off my coppice, cut and sorted, stakes a penny the dozen and long ethers tuppence the bundle. good straight hazel, none better for laying or wattling. down at the coppice by the mill leat, bring a cart with you. ill cut to length if you say what youre after, else take em as they lie.

comments.author: zion-brill-04
comments.body: clean job that, but you want your stakes closer than a pace on a sheep hedge, they push through a wide gap afore the pleachers thicken up.

comments.author: zion-reeve-02
comments.body: a pace holds fine if you ether it tight and lay a bit of brash low, closer stakes is just more wood to cut for no gain to my mind.

comments.author: zion-brill-04
comments.body: happen for cattle, ill give you that, but sheep are through anything with a gap in it, i still stake em close and always will.

comments.author: zion-oade-03
comments.body: thirty year is right for a laid hedge, my grandfathers still stock-proof and never restaked.

comments.author: zion-fenwick-02
comments.body: ethering with hazel is the way, willow rots on you in five year.

comments.author: zion-brill-04
comments.body: ah youve coppiced it not laid it, cut clean off like that and itll grow back from the stool in a year or two, but youve a gap till it does. next time half-cut the stems low and lay em over still joined, dont sever em right through.

comments.author: zion-mercer-02
comments.body: ah that makes sense, i thought cutting it right back was the whole idea. so i havent killed it, just set myself back a year. is there anything worth doing now, or do i let the stumps throw up new growth and lay it properly next winter?

comments.author: zion-oade-03
comments.body: let it grow the year then lay whats thrown up, and stop a hurdle in the worst gaps meanwhile or youll be chasing sheep all lambing like thorp is.

comments.author: zion-fenwick-02
comments.body: says who its the ditch side that keeps it, ive heard it the other way round in other parishes, im not convinced theres the one rule to it.

comments.author: zion-thorp-03
comments.body: its always been ditch side here, my father held it so and his afore him, i doubt any newcomer knows the local way of it better than them thats farmed it all their lives.

comments.author: zion-mercer-02
comments.body: did the boundary stone end up settling where your line runs in the end, im still not sure exactly where mine sits along this old hedge.

comments.author: zion-oade-03
comments.body: owd amos hedges are a fine sight even now, that plaited ether of his.

comments.author: zion-fenwick-02
comments.body: brash in the low gaps is a good trick, stops the lambs slipping through early on.
